_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot)
_meta.note: Federal AZ batch (AZ-01 R/D candidates + AZ-02 Crane + AZ-03 Ansari + AZ-04 Stanton). Galvin DECLINED the 2026 AZ-01 race 2025-09-30 — status fix. Stanton: Laken Riley YES cross-party deviation + Chair NDC Immigration & Border Security Task Force. Crane: HARD on HR2/HR5/HR26/FISA/foreign-aid NO. Ansari: pro-Israel hawk lean (DMFI-endorsed, ICC sanctions YES) atop full-progressive domestic record. McCartney: Term Limits pledge + 'smart border security' framing + critical of Netanyahu — D-orthodox on life/marriage/family.

profile.religion: Protestant / Christian (Pew Research 118th Cong list; no specific denomination publicly stated)
profile.background: U.S. Representative AZ-02 (R) since Jan 2023. Navy SEAL veteran, SEAL Team 3, five deployments incl. Iraq (2001-2014). Founder of Bottle Breacher (.50-cal-shell bottle openers, sold 2022). Heritage Action lifetime 93% — top-tier hardline conservative.

eli-crane.notes_set: U.S. Representative AZ-02 (R), first elected 2022. [2026-06 federal refinement] Strong America-First / deficit-hawk / civil-libertarian profile. HARD evidence: YES H.R.26 Born-Alive Survivors (1/11/2023); YES H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights (3/24/2023) + offered Crane amendment for parental private right of action; YES H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (5/11/2023); YES Laken Riley Act (2025); NO Fiscal Responsibility Act 2023 (one of 71 R hardliners against the McCarthy-Biden debt deal); one of 8 R to oust McCarthy as Speaker (10/3/2023); one of only 13 House members to vote NO on ALL four bills in the April 2024 foreign-aid package (Israel, Ukraine, Taiwan, REPO/TikTok); cosponsor H.R.6570 Protect Liberty & End Warrantless Surveillance Act + Crane-Biggs FISA 702 warrant-requirement amendment. Heritage Action 119th 92% / lifetime 93%. AIPAC career-total only $500 (per TrackAIPAC). 2026 race: incumbent_seeking_reelection; D challenger Jonathan Nez (rematch) + Eric Descheenie; no serious R primary challenger. Religion: Protestant / Christian — no specific church publicly stated.

0.note: Voted YES on H.R.26 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (1/11/2023, 220-210).
Voted YES on H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (3/24/2023, 213-208) — universal parental-rights framework for K-12.

1.note: H.R.5 mandates parental notification on medical/mental-health interventions for minors; Crane offered a strengthening amendment adding a parental private right of action (failed 61-365).

2.note: H.R.5 explicitly targets CRT/SOGI/gender-ideology curricula in K-12; Crane voted YES.
Voted NO on the Fiscal Responsibility Act 2023 (debt-ceiling deal) — one of 71 R hardliners citing inadequate spending cuts; Heritage Action 119th 92% / lifetime 93%.

0.note: Voted YES on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (5/11/2023, 219-213) — border wall funding, asylum restrictions, mandatory detention.

1.note: Voted YES on Laken Riley Act (2025) — mandatory ICE detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crimes.

0.note: Voted NO on all four bills in the April 2024 foreign-aid package (one of only 13 House members); demanded Article I war-powers discipline.

1.note: Voted to cut all military aid to Ukraine (2023) and to ban cluster munitions to Ukraine; America-First / no-forever-wars posture.

3.note: Per TrackAIPAC, has accepted $500 career-total from AIPAC/pro-Israel lobby PACs — a very small amount but technically not zero; scored FALSE per strict rubric reading.

profile.religion: Agnostic — publicly self-identified; member, Congressional Freethought Caucus ('I respect everyone's faith, but I do not personally practice one.')
profile.background: U.S. Representative AZ-03 (D) since Jan 2025; succeeded Ruben Gallego (now Senator). First-term member; Iranian-American (parents fled post-Islamic-Revolution Iran). Stanford BA Int'l Relations; Cambridge MPhil (Gates Cambridge Scholar). Former Phoenix City Council District 7 (2021-2024) and 2023 Phoenix Vice Mayor. Senior policy advisor to UN Secretaries-General Ban Ki-moon and Antonio Guterres on the Paris Agreement / climate.

yassamin-ansari.notes_set: U.S. Representative AZ-03 (D), first-term, since Jan 2025. [2026-06 federal refinement] Full-progressive domestic record with ONE notable pro-Israel-hawk cross-party deviation. HARD evidence: original cosponsor H.R.15 Equality Act (119th, 4/29/2025); cosponsor Transgender Bill of Rights (Jayapal/Markey, 2/11/2026); cosponsor H.R.5361 George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (2025); cosponsor H.R.14 John R. Lewis VRA (2025); cosponsor American Dream and Promise Act; introduced ICE-accountability package + called for ICE abolition Jan 2026. Heritage Action lifetime 0%. Planned Parenthood Action Fund + EMILY's List + GIFFORDS PAC endorsed. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION ZONE — Israel: VOTED YES on H.R.23 sanctioning the ICC (Jan 2025, joined GOP majority; minority of House Dems); DMFI PAC endorsed her 2024 primary; opposed Sanders' Nov 2024 resolution to block $20B arms sales to Israel; supports Abraham Accords expansion. ON LAKEN RILEY: voted NO (Jan 22 2025), the only AZ D to break with Gallego/Kelly/Hobbs — to the LEFT, not a conservative deviation. NOT a Squad member; member of Congressional Progressive Caucus, Asian Pacific American Caucus, Freethought Caucus. Phoenix Council record: backed civilian police oversight (Office of Accountability and Transparency) but did NOT push defund-police. 2026 race: faces Sandy Cano-Bravo in D primary; R primary: Kirt Burgess.

4.note: Endorsed and supported by Planned Parenthood Action Fund and EMILY's List — both abortion-industry PACs.

0.note: Original cosponsor H.R.15 Equality Act (119th Cong., 4/29/2025) — codifies sexual-orientation/gender-identity protections in civil rights law (functionally rejects man-woman exclusive marriage as legally privileged).

2.note: Cosponsor Transgender Bill of Rights (Jayapal/Markey, 2/11/2026) — affirms gender identity as a legal status equal to sex.

4.note: Cosponsor Equality Act expanding SOGI mandates into schools, public accommodations, and federal-grant recipients.

1.note: Cosponsor Transgender Bill of Rights — includes access to gender-affirming care for minors without parental-consent thresholds.

0.note: Member, Congressional Freethought Caucus (secular-government caucus); publicly self-identifies as agnostic — secular-public-square orientation.

2.note: Heritage Action lifetime score 0%; consistent vote with progressive-spending bloc against fiscal-restraint amendments.

1.note: Public opposition to SAVE-Act-style voter-ID-with-citizenship-verification bills ('Republican voter suppression').

3.note: Cosponsor H.R.14 John R. Lewis VRA 2025 — restores DOJ preclearance and federally protects mass mail-in / drop-box access.

1.note: Voted NO on S.5 Laken Riley Act (1/22/2025, Roll Call 23) — only AZ D to break with Gallego/Kelly/Hobbs; also called for ICE abolition (Jan 2026) and introduced ICE-accountability package.

2.note: Cosponsor American Dream and Promise Act — codifies DACA; explicit opposition to federal preemption of sanctuary policies.

1.note: GIFFORDS PAC endorsed on platform of assault-weapons ban, age 21, universal background checks, red-flag laws.

2.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YES on H.R.23 sanctioning the ICC over Israeli-leaders arrest warrants (Jan 2025); opposed Sanders' Nov 2024 resolution to block $20B in arms sales to Israel; supports continued military aid to Israel without additional conditions — pro-Israel-aid hawk lean.

profile.religion: Catholic — raised at St. Jerome Catholic Church (NW Phoenix); parents ran Christian Needs Network
profile.background: U.S. Representative AZ-04 (D) since Jan 2019 (originally AZ-09, redistricted to AZ-04 in 2023). Former Phoenix Mayor (2012-2018), AZ Deputy AG (2009-2011), Phoenix City Council (2000-2009). Marquette undergrad (Truman Scholar); Univ. of Michigan Law. New Democrat Coalition member; CHAIR, NDC Immigration & Border Security Task Force. Committees: T&I, Foreign Affairs, Select Committee on the CCP.

greg-stanton.notes_set: U.S. Representative AZ-04 (D), since Jan 2019. [2026-06 federal refinement] Center-left New Democrat with TWO defining cross-party deviations: (1) Laken Riley Act YES (Jan 7 2025), one of 48 D crossovers — chairs the New Democrat Coalition Immigration & Border Security Task Force, led bipartisan border trip with Rep. Ciscomani, blasted Senate Republicans for blocking the bipartisan border deal. Now being primaried from the LEFT (Kai Newkirk, AZ Dem Party Progressive Council co-chair) citing the Laken Riley vote. (2) Pro-Israel hawk: DMFI PAC endorsed 2026; AIPAC career-total $314,435 (TrackAIPAC); joined bipartisan resolution reaffirming Israel support (Oct 2023); opposed conditional aid to Israel, demanded clean funding package. Otherwise D-orthodox: cosponsor WHPA + Right to Contraception + IVF / STOP Comstock; YES Respect for Marriage Act (Roll Call 2022/373 + 2022/513) + cosponsor Equality Act; NO on H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights (2023/161); YES Inflation Reduction Act; YES Assault Weapons Ban of 2022 (Roll Call 2022/410); YES George Floyd Justice in Policing Act; pro-HR1/Freedom to Vote. As Phoenix Mayor (Feb 2016) voted to END public invocations at Council, replacing with moment of silence (post-Satanic Temple invocation request) — cited First Amendment compelling all-or-none. Heritage Action lifetime 5% (118th) / 0% (117th, 116th). Catholic (St. Jerome, NW Phoenix). 2026 race: D primary 7/21/2026 vs Kai Newkirk (left challenge).

0.note: Cosponsor Women's Health Protection Act + Right to Contraception Act + Access to Family Building Act (IVF) + STOP Comstock Act; supports codifying Roe.
Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (H.R.8404) both July 2022 passage (Roll Call 373) and Dec 2022 final passage (Roll Call 513).

1.note: Public statement celebrating RFMA passage as 'protecting marriage equality' — explicit support for same-sex marriage in federal law.

4.note: Cosponsor and YES vote Equality Act 2019; HRC endorsed; as Phoenix Mayor delivered HRC Municipal Equality Index 100/100.

0.note: Voted NO on H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (3/24/2023, party-line); bill passed 213-208 with all Dems opposed.

3.note: As Phoenix Mayor (Feb 2016) voted to END public invocations at City Council meetings, replacing with a moment of silence (post-Satanic-Temple-invocation request); argued First Amendment compelled all-faiths-or-none.

2.note: Heritage Action lifetime 5% (118th); voted YES on Inflation Reduction Act (Aug 2022) — added trillions in spending; opposes balanced-budget posture.

1.note: Vocal supporter H.R.1 / For the People Act and H.R.5746 Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act — opposes voter-ID-with-citizenship-verification.

3.note: Supports federal mandate for mass mail-in voting access and drop-box availability via Freedom to Vote Act advocacy.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YES on Laken Riley Act (1/7/2025, 264-159) — one of 48 House Democrats joining the GOP majority; mandates ICE detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crimes.

2.note: Chairs NDC Immigration & Border Security Task Force; led bipartisan border trip with Rep. Ciscomani (R-AZ); endorsed bipartisan Senate border deal as 'toughest, most comprehensive in decades' — but no specific federal-preemption-on-sanctuary roll call located. Null pending cited vote.

1.note: Voted YES + cosponsor H.R.1808 Assault Weapons Ban of 2022 (Roll Call 410, 217-213 on 7/29/2022).

2.note: DMFI PAC endorsed (2026); joined bipartisan resolution reaffirming US support for Israel (Oct 2023, 390 votes + Foreign Affairs leadership); demanded clean Israel funding package without conditions.

3.note: AIPAC career-total $314,435 in pro-Israel-lobby PAC contributions per TrackAIPAC.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (DECLINED 2025-09-30 — remains sitting Maricopa County Supervisor District 2, Chair 2025)

profile.religion: Likely Catholic (Holy Cross + Notre Dame Law education) — no explicit personal faith statement publicly documented
profile.background: Maricopa County Supervisor District 2 since Dec 2021 (appointed to vacancy), full term elected Nov 2022, re-elected Nov 2024. 2025 Chair of Maricopa BOS (Kate Brophy McGee took 2026 gavel). Partner at Rose Law Group (land use, real estate, water law); Holy Cross BA, Notre Dame Law JD. Declined federal AZ-01 race 2025-09-30 same day Schweikert pivoted to governor.

thomas-galvin.notes_set: STATUS FIX (2026-06-02): DECLINED 2026 AZ-01 federal bid. On Sept 30 2025 (same day Schweikert vacated AZ-01 for governor), Galvin posted on X: 'I have the best job in Arizona government and have no interest in spending time away from my family. I'm sure a great conservative will emerge to carry on David's legacy.' FOX10 confirmed 'Maricopa Co. Supervisor Thomas Galvin declines congressional bid.' Wikipedia 2026 AZ House elections page lists him under 'Declined.' Remains sitting Maricopa BOS D2 (Chair 2025). Documented BOS record: HARD pro-school-choice (self-describes as Arizona's biggest ESA advocate, his sons are ESA beneficiaries); HARD pro-sheriff/anti-DOJ-oversight (pushing to end the Melendres consent decree on MCSO); HARD pro-border-security rhetoric (joined Arizona border-security org Aug 2025). ELECTION-INTEGRITY POSTURE — institutionalist Republican lane (Bill Gates / Stephen Richer): voted to certify 2022 election (11/28/2022); defendant in Kari Lake's 2022 lawsuit; defended drop boxes against MAGA-aligned Recorder Justin Heap (May 2026, 4-1) calling election re-litigation 'the 2020 election hamster wheel'; proposed reform to count 95% of votes on election night (move up early-ballot cutoff, eliminate emergency Sat/Mon voting). Drew MAGA criticism for nominating Schweikert's 2022 D challenger Jevin Hodge to fill an LD-8 House vacancy (Jan 2024). Saudi-alfalfa-lobbying conflict: as Rose Law Group attorney represented Fondomonte Arizona (Saudi groundwater pumping). No SBA Pro-Life / Center for Arizona Policy / Trump / NRA endorsements located. Pro-life and pro-2A self-labels exist (campaign site) but are unsourced beyond the candidate bio.

0.note: Self-describes: 'no bigger advocate in Arizona's Legislature than me when it comes to school choice and empowering parents'; his sons are AZ ESA (Empowerment Scholarship Account) beneficiaries.

Aug 2025: joined Arizona border-security organization; quote: 'Arizona has long been impacted by prior decades of failed border policies in Washington D.C. resulting in a flood of criminal activity pouring across the border... smuggling of illicit drugs and human trafficking reach well beyond our border communities.'

3.note: May 2026: led 4-1 BOS majority defending Maricopa election drop boxes against MAGA-aligned Recorder Justin Heap; quote: 'this is shocking and appalling... we are stuck in this hamster wheel of the 2020 election' — institutionalist-Republican election lane, opposes the drop-box-skeptic position.

set.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (2026 D primary candidate · open seat · Schweikert vacancy)

profile.religion: No public statement of religious affiliation (Fordham University grad — Jesuit Catholic institution — but no personal faith stated)
profile.background: Founder/publisher In Business Magazine (Greater Phoenix); President/CEO InMedia Company. Chair Maricopa County Workforce Development Board. Board Chair one•n•ten (Arizona's largest nonprofit serving LGBTQ+ youth). Openly gay, married to husband Korwyn, son Camron. Fordham University grad.

rick-mccartney-az-01.notes_set: CANDIDACY VERIFIED (2026-06-02): on the 2026 AZ-01 D primary ballot (July 21 2026). FEC committee active, ~$467,698 raised / ~$355,130 COH through Q3 2025 (incl. $250K candidate self-loan). Active campaign site + Instagram + LinkedIn. [2026-06 federal refinement, social-media protocol] D-orthodox on life/marriage/family with two notable cross-party deviations and modest center-left framing: HARD on (1) codify Roe federally, support abortion-medication access + interstate travel; (2) pass the Equality Act, 'protect trans youth'; (3) restore VRA, end partisan gerrymandering; (4) impeach Trump/Noem when warranted. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS / NOTABLE: signed U.S. Term Limits pledge (3 House terms / 2 Senate terms) — unusual for a D primary candidate; 'smart border security' framing alongside pathway to citizenship + Dreamer protections (moderate-D triangulation for an R-leaning swing district); 'supports strong U.S.-Israel relationship' BUT 'critical of Netanyahu-era policies and far-right governance' / opposes 'blank check' (Israel-critical-left lean). NO HARD evidence on guns, taxes, ESA, voter ID, vaccine mandates, police funding, or Iran posture — null those cells. No major national org endorsements (DCCC / EMILY's List / LGBTQ Victory Fund / Equality PAC); endorsement bench is local AZ Dems plus Mike Schmuhl (former Buttigieg campaign manager) — signals New Democrat / Buttigieg-world lane. Polling well behind Amish Shah and Jonathan Treble per HighGround AZ poll.

0.note: Platform statement: 'government has no business interfering in personal medical decisions'; supports codifying Roe v. Wade into federal law, abortion medication access, and interstate travel for abortion services.
Openly gay, married to husband Korwyn; raises son Camron — personally rejects man-woman exclusive marriage.

2.note: Platform: 'fight to protect trans youth and ensure safety and dignity' — affirms transgender identity and opposes restrictions.

4.note: Explicit support for passing the Equality Act expanding LGBTQ+ protections in civil rights law.

1.note: Platform: 'fight to protect trans youth' — opposes parental-notification thresholds on gender-related interventions.
Platform: 'restoring the Voting Rights Act, ending partisan gerrymandering' — opposes SAVE-Act-style voter-ID-with-citizenship-verification framework.

0.note: Platform supports 'smart border security' + 'secure, humane, and functional immigration system' — moderate-D framing but no explicit position on physical barrier; null.

1.note: Platform supports pathway to citizenship + Dreamer protections + fixing legal backlogs — opposes mandatory deportation framework.

2.note: Platform 'supports strong U.S.-Israel relationship' BUT 'critical of Netanyahu-era policies and far-right governance' / opposes 'blank check' — mixed signal on aid-to-allies-criticized-for-Christian-persecution test; null.
